更新，美国东部标准时间下午12:30：今天，在向白宫记者进行简报之初，新闻秘书詹·普萨基（Jen Psaki）就Artemis计划发表了以下声明：
 ＆＃34;通过Artemis计划，美国将与业界和国际伙伴合作，将宇航员送上月球表面，另一个男人和一个女人登上月球，这非常令人兴奋，从而开展新的令人兴奋的科学，未来前往火星的任务，并展示美国的价值观。迄今为止，半个世纪前，只有12个人在月球上行走。 Artemis计划是通往火星的起点，它提供了向其添加数字的机会。探月活动在国会得到了广泛和双头的支持，最近在2021财年综合支出法案中有详细说明，当然我们支持这项努力和努力。＆＃34;
 该声明之所以引人注目，是因为它显然是在拜登政府内的科学官员向普萨奇（Psaki）通报之后，并反映了他们对阿耳for弥斯计划的总体目标的支持。没有详细信息，但是新主管部门希望在有关空间等主题上做到这一点。当然，时间和方式也会发生变化。但最重要的是：为Artemis计划而战。
 原始故事：拜登政府领导下有关太空政策的最大问题之一是，总统是否会接受特朗普白宫提出的阿耳emi弥斯月球计划。该计划要求人类返回月球并随着月球基地的建立而增加。前副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）为2024年首次登陆月球设定了积极的时间表。
 很久以来就很清楚，这个时间表是无法实现的，棺材的最后钉子是在12月，当时国会仅在2021财年预算中为人类着陆系统提供了8.5亿美元。这仅是美国宇航局表示希望有希望实现2024年着陆日期的四分之一。
     由于资金水平中等，目前尚不清楚国会对阿尔of弥斯计划的支持程度。但是在星期三，11名民主党参议员给拜登政府发了一封信，敦促对人类登陆系统计划的支持，该计划是使人类重返月球所需的关键硬件。
 ＆＃34;开发下一代载人月球着陆器是半个世纪以来首次将宇航员送回月球的必不可少的一步，其中包括使第一名妇女登上月球的历史性里程碑，＆＃34;声明这封信。 
这封信似乎是受到美国宇航局决定推迟从目前正在研发月球着陆器的三支队伍中选拔一事的决定所激发的。一个由Blue Origin领导，第二个由Dynetics领导，第三个由SpaceX领导。信中的签字人包括来自Blue Origin国家队的几个州的参议员。包括科罗拉多州和华盛顿州。毫不奇怪，这封信强调了该计划将产生的工作。
 在从去年开始的10个月的基准期间（每个团队都在细化提案的细节）之后，美国宇航局本月将选择两个提案进行开发。但是，该机构上周表示，将这一决定推迟60天进行进一步评估。这可能是因为NASA希望给拜登政府更多的时间来确定其在Artemis上的发展道路。
 参议员们写道，美国宇航局应该坚持其最初的时间表。 ＆＃34;敦促您继续进行计划中的选择，并在您的2022财年预算请求中包括（人类着陆系统）所有必要的资金，＆＃34;信中说。
 令人奇怪的是，参议员们没有承认是国会未能在今年提供白宫为登月者提供的全部资金，而不是相反。但是，尽管如此，这似乎是参议院将支持继续进行的着陆器计划的强烈意图。
     拜登（Biden）政府刚上任仅两个星期，任职期很长。因此，也许还不足为奇的是，没有任命管理员的提名人，也没有详细说明空间政策。这些细节通常不会在新政府中提早公布。现在需要对登月着陆器做出决定（或平底船），这将使我们更早地了解拜登政府对人类航天的意图。
 在周三在白宫举行的新闻发布会上，FOX新闻克里斯汀·费舍尔（Kristin Fisher）问到空间。 （费舍尔（Fisher）在这个问题上很有作为；她是两个航天飞机宇航员的女儿）。她的问题之一是询问新闻秘书詹·普萨基（Jen Psaki）关于Artemis计划以及拜登政府在该问题上的立场。
 ＆＃34;我个人对太空感兴趣，＆＃34;普萨基回应。我认为这是一个有趣的研究领域。但我尚未与我们的团队讨论这个特定程序。＆＃34;她承诺将获得更多信息并与费舍尔进行跟进。截至周四上午，我们尚未看到其他信息。 
如果拜登总统想以两党的方式接近太空，那么这里就有一条前进的道路。尽管这封信来自民主党，但国会中仍有许多共和党人支持特朗普政府概述的阿耳emi弥斯计划。每个人（包括参议员，他们在信中均未提及登陆日期）可能会同意，在当前预算下2026年或2028年的登月更为现实，只需要适度增加。
 ＆＃34;如果拜登政府愿意，提议增加NASA的规模以容纳Artemis并对其科学计划进行新的投资，将是拜登政府轻松获得的政治胜利。＆＃34;行星学会的Casey Dreier告诉Ars。
 那么现在会发生什么呢？拜登政府可能会被迫加快其在太空政策方面的时间表，而这只是一批新员工刚刚入驻的时候–代理参谋长Bhavya Lal于上周刚被任命后所有。最终，明智的选择是，尽管可以对Artemis计划进​​行一定程度的修改，但它很可能会继续进行。
note_en:
Update, 12:30pm EST: Today, at the outset of her briefing with White House reporters, press secretary Jen Psaki offered  the following statement on the Artemis Program:
 "Through the Artemis Program, the United States will work with industry and international partners to send astronauts to the surface of the Moon—another man and a woman to the Moon, which is very exciting—conduct new and exciting science, prepare for future missions to Mars, and demonstrate America's values. To date, only 12 humans have walked on the Moon— that was half a century ago. The Artemis Program, a waypoint to Mars, provides the opportunity to add numbers to that. Lunar exploration has broad and bicameral support in Congress, most recently detailed in the FY2021 omnibus spending bill, and certainly we support this effort and endeavor."
 The statement is notable because it clearly comes after Psaki was briefed by science officials within the Biden administration and reflects their support for the general thrust of the Artemis Program. Details are nonexistent, but that's to be expected from a new administration on a topic such as space. And there will certainly be changes in timing and approach. But the bottom line is this:  Game on for the Artemis Program.
 Original story: One of the biggest questions about space policy under the Biden administration is whether the president will embrace the Artemis Moon program set into motion by the Trump White House. This plan called for a return of humans to the Moon and the buildup over time of a lunar base. Former Vice President Mike Pence  set an aggressive timeline for the first Moon landing to occur—2024.
 It has been clear for many months that this timeline was unattainable, and the final nail in the coffin came in December, when Congress provided just $850 million for a Human Landing System in the fiscal year 2021 budget. This is only one-quarter of what NASA said it needed to have any hope of making the 2024 landing date.
     Because of this middling funding level, it was not clear how supportive Congress was of the Artemis plan. But on Wednesday, 11 Democratic senators sent a letter to the Biden administration urging support for the Human Landing System program, which is the critical hardware needed to enable a human return to the Moon.
 "Developing the next generation crewed lunar lander is an essential step in returning astronauts to the Moon for the first time in half a century, including the historic milestone of landing the first woman on the Moon,"  states the letter.
  The letter appears to have been spurred by NASA's decision to delay a down-select of  three teams currently working to develop lunar landers; one is led by Blue Origin, a second by Dynetics, and a third by SpaceX. Signatories from the letter include senators from several states represented by Blue Origin's "National Team," including Colorado and Washington. Not surprisingly, the letter emphasizes the jobs this program will yield.
 After a 10-month base period that began last year, during which each team refined details of their proposals, NASA was due to choose two proposals this month to move into development. However, last week the agency said it was delaying that decision for 60 days for additional evaluation. This is likely because NASA wanted to give the Biden administration more time to determine its path forward on Artemis.
 The senators wrote that NASA should stick to its original timeline. "We urge you to proceed with the planned selection and to include all necessary funding for (the Human Landing System) in your FY 2022 budget request," the letter said.
 It is curious that the senators do not acknowledge that it was Congress who failed to provide the full funding requested by the White House for lunar landers this year, not the other way around. But all the same, this seems like a fairly strong statement of intent that the Senate will support the lander program going forward.
     The Biden administration has barely been in office for two weeks, and it has a lot on its plate. So perhaps it's not surprising that a nominee for administrator has not yet been named, nor has a space policy been outlined in detail. Such details typically do not come out early in new administrations. The need to now make (or punt) on a decision on the lunar landers will offer us an early glimpse of the Biden administration's intentions toward human spaceflight.
 During Wednesday's press briefing at the White House, FOX News' Kristin Fisher asked about space. ( Fisher has cred on the subject; she's the daughter of two space shuttle astronauts). One of her questions asked Press Secretary Jen Psaki about the Artemis program and where the Biden administration stood on the subject.
 "I am personally interested in space," Psaki responded. "I think it's a fascinating area of study. But I have not spoken with our team about this particular program." She promised to get more information and follow-up with Fisher. As of Thursday morning, we've yet to see additional information.
  There is a path forward here for President Biden if he wants to approach space in a bipartisan manner. Although this letter came from Democrats, there are plenty of Republicans in Congress who support the Artemis plan outlined by the Trump administration. Everyone (including the senators, who did not mention a landing date in their letter) would probably agree that a 2026 or a 2028 Moon landing is more realistic under the current budget, with only modest increases necessary.
 "Proposing to grow NASA to accommodate both Artemis and a renewed investment in its science programs would be an easy political win for the Biden administration if they wish to take it." Casey Dreier of The Planetary Society told Ars.
 So what happens now? It's likely that the Biden administration will be forced to accelerate its timeline on space policy at a time when a slate of new hires is only just settling in—an acting chief of staff,  Bhavya Lal, was just named last week after all. Ultimately, the smart bet is that while the Artemis program may be modified to some extent, it could very well continue.

大约3200年前，在埃及，古代的防油工将木乃伊包裹在干泥中，以修复粗心的盗墓者所造成的破坏。考古学家最近使用CT扫描仪解开了死者故事的一部分。该研究发表在《 PLOS ONE》杂志上，揭示了一种未知的木乃伊化技术，以及一个关于抢劫，家庭奉献和错误身份的奇怪故事。
 该人现在仅被称为NMR.27.3，死于相对年轻。死者的名字已不复存在，其性别尚有待商later（稍后再讨论）。死后，强盗至少两次闯入坟墓，现在考古学家将故事的某些片段拼凑在一起-多数是事后的章节。
 所留下的是古埃及罕见的生与死的一瞥。这位不愿透露姓名的木乃伊匿名人士透露，即使死后数年，活着的亲戚仍然对死者足够关心，实际上在强盗抢劫者损坏尸体后对其进行了修复。为了修复木乃伊，古老的防腐剂在亚麻布上抹上泥浆，以帮助保持身体的形状，这是现代考古学家从未见过的技术。
 在CT扫描中，麦格理大学的考古学家Karin Sowada和她的同事们发现，该人死亡后，包括左膝盖和小腿的骨头在内的几处骨头都被打断了。骨骼在死亡后的干燥和脆性断裂与在生命中的断裂不同。尽管骨头断裂了，有些甚至被弄断了，但直接覆盖在骨折处的亚麻包裹物却整齐，不受干扰，干泥的薄层也没有破裂。这表明破坏者在破坏后重新包裹了木乃伊。
  对于古埃及人而言，木乃伊的整个目的是为了将尸体保存到永远，作为人的灵魂的船只（如果我们是技术上的人，至少是灵魂的一部分），否则就没有了。死者无法在来世生存。没有压力或任何东西。
 法老塞提一世（公元前1323-1279年）和阿蒙霍特普三世（公元前1411-1353年）都在死后很长一段时间都进行了重新包装和重新包装-对于塞提一世来说不止一次。但是法老倾向于建造精巧的仪庙，并保留祭司几个世纪以来一直在密切关注他们的坟墓。
 对于不是神的世俗化身的每个人，活着的家庭成员可能只检查墓葬，最多只能供几代人使用。但是很明显，“他们或与死者有联系的其他人对死者的遗体状况有足够的关注，后来在尸体受到骚扰和肢解后，他们投资了涂有泥灰泥和油漆的甲壳，” Sowada和她的同事写道。 
我们不知道这个人的名字，但是这些遗骸无声地证明了他们的家人对自己的来世和对祖先的义务的重视程度。遗体的事后不幸事件也提醒我们，抢劫是一项古老的职业。
  为了修复抢劫过程中造成的损坏，防腐剂将亚麻包裹物重新缠绕，然后在布上抹上一层薄薄的泥浆，在损坏的木乃伊周围形成一个保护壳。干燥后，泥浆有助于将折断的肢体固定在适当位置，并保持完整的身体形状。在泥壳上再加几层亚麻布，然后死了，死者又好又新，可以继续来世。
 考古学家以前从未见过这种技术，这是Sowada于1999年在一次CT扫描会议上首次注意到薄薄的泥浆时引起人们的注意的。二十年后，CT扫描技术的改进使Sowada和她的同事们受益匪浅。以获得更详细的外观。最近的扫描分辨率使考古学家能够区分亚麻的各个层之间以及亚麻与涂漆的泥壳之间。结果证实，他们正在寻找一种木乃伊把戏，几千年来没人见过。
 几千年来，木乃伊的基本原理并未发生太大变化：去除粘稠的部分，使身体干燥，涂抹防腐剂，然后将身体包裹在亚麻中以帮助保存。但是，就像任何技术和/或文化实践一样，细节随着时间的流逝而变化，并且它们也根据您的居住地以及您的富裕程度和重要性而变化。
 在第21王朝（公元前1294年至945年）前后，一些皇家木乃伊包括一层浸有树脂的亚麻布，然后将其干燥成硬壳。 Seti I和他的儿子Rameses II都接受了这种治疗。索瓦达和她的同事们认为，涂上一层淤泥本来可以是富裕但并非蓝血统的人，试图复制上流社会。但是，如果是这种情况，很难理解为什么防腐剂会保存它以进行维修。也许他们想起了树脂的技术和思想，对于这种尸体修复来说，干成硬壳的东西是完美的选择，但是在天文上要比树脂便宜些吗？
 至少在某些社会阶层中，在一段时间内将木乃伊包裹在泥泞中也是一种标准做法。考古学家还没有足够的信息要知道，但是Sowada和她的同事怀疑NMR.27.3并不是唯一接受这种治疗的木乃伊，只是第一位考古学家注意到。
 索瓦达和她的同事写道：“要了解这种做法在新王国后期如何普遍，将需要进行放射学研究并发表来自这个时代的其他非王室木乃伊个体，” 
然而，可以肯定的是：NMR.27.3绝对不是名叫Meruah的新王国贵族，死于公元前1000年左右。她的名字和头衔都画在木制棺材的盖子上，但是里面木乃伊的尸体实际上并不是Meruah。放射性碳年代测定法和棺材上画的场景风格表明她死于公元前945年至1040年之间。另一方面，木乃伊亚麻布包裹的放射性碳数据表明，棺材内的人在公元前1207年至1113年之间死亡。 Meruah甚至可能还没有出生。
 考古学家可能永远不会找到Meruah的尸体，但他们很确定一个陌生人最终如何进入她的棺材：殖民主义。
 在过去的某个时候，抢劫者可能闯入了Meruah的坟墓，并偷走了她的棺材。后来-更晚了-阴暗的19世纪古物贩子们决定，如果里面有木乃伊，他们可以为高档棺材加价，于是他们找到了。显然，这是对的，因为一位名叫查尔斯·尼科尔森爵士的富裕英国游客在1856年前往埃及的旅途中购买了梅鲁亚的棺材和不知名的人。
  现在正在占据Meruah棺木的人可能是女性，也可能不是女性。几年前，从遗体中测序得到的DNA暗示该人是男性，但是Sowada和她的同事说，在他们的CT扫描中，该人的骨盆，头骨和下颌看起来更像女人的骨头。
 Sowada告诉Ars：“我们假设骨骼检查结果和aDNA结果的差异源于aDNA研究的问题。” “未经进一步调查，我们假设问题与流程中某处的污染有关。”
 涂上的泥浆本身只会增加不确定性。 Sowada和她的同事们使用X射线荧光和拉曼光谱检查了泥壳的碎片，这两种技术都是根据样品分子反射光或X射线的方式来测量其化学成分的。原来是细粒棕色粘土，上面涂有白色方解石颜料，然后涂成红色。
 尽管泥土覆盖了木乃伊整个人的身体，但Sowada和她的同事检查的样品却来自头部附近。 Sowada的一位同事在1999年小心翼翼地去除了三个小碎片。Sowada和她的同事们还不知道贝壳的其余部分是否也被粉刷过。 “我很想知道整个甲壳是否被涂漆甚至装饰过；我们将不得不等待成像技术的进一步改进来回答这个问题。” Sowada告诉Ars。 
从19世纪到21世纪（大约NMR.27.3逝世），埃及艺术家倾向于将男人的脸涂成红色，将女人的脸涂成黄色。 那只是做事的方式，埃及艺术往往非常传统和风格化。 死亡面具和棺材遵循传统，男人的脸上涂有淡红色颜料，如NMR.27.3外壳上的颜料。  但是，在现代卢克索附近的底比斯（Thebes），考古学家发现了一些描绘了较深的红棕色皮肤的女性例子。 由于Meruah的棺材是Theban风格的，而Nicholson则在卢克索附近购买了棺材和木乃伊，因此红色颜料也可能将死者标记为女性。 毕竟，约定会根据您的时间和位置而变化。
note_en:
Approximately 3,200 years ago in Egypt, ancient embalmers encased a mummy in dried mud to repair the damage done by careless tomb robbers. Archaeologists recently used a CT scanner to unravel part of the dead person’s story. The study, published in the journal PLOS ONE, revealed an unknown mummification technique, along with a strange tale of grave robbing, family devotion, and mistaken identity.
 The person, now known only as NMR.27.3, died relatively young. The name of the deceased is lost to history, and their gender is debatable (more on that later). After death, grave robbers broke into their tomb at least twice, and now archaeologists have pieced together some fragments of the story—mostly the postmortem chapters.
 What is left behind is a rare glimpse of life and death in ancient Egypt. The anonymous mummified person reveals that even years after death, living relatives still cared enough about the deceased to actually have the corpse repaired (sort of) after grave robbers damaged it. And to repair the mummy, ancient embalmers plastered mud over the linen wrappings to help the body hold its shape, a technique that modern archaeologists have never seen before.
 In the CT scan, Macquarie University archaeologists Karin Sowada and her colleagues could see that several bones, including the left knee and the bones of the lower leg, had been broken after the person died; bone breaks differently after death, when it’s dry and brittle, than during life. Although the bones were broken, and some were even disarticulated, the linen wrappings directly over the fractures were neat and undisturbed, and the thin layer of dried mud wasn’t cracked. That suggests the embalmers re-wrapped the mummy after the damage was done.
  For ancient Egyptians, the whole point of mummification was to preserve the corpse for all eternity as a vessel for the person's soul (at least one part of the soul, if we're being technical), without which the deceased couldn't survive in the afterlife. No pressure or anything.
 Pharaohs Seti I (1323-1279 BCE) and Amenhotep III (1411-1353 BCE) both underwent re-packing and re-wrapping long after they died—more than once for Seti I. But pharaohs tended to have elaborate mortuary temples with priests keeping a close eye on their tombs for centuries.
 For everyone who wasn’t the earthly incarnation of a god, living family members probably only checked on the tomb and made offerings for a couple of generations at most. But apparently, “They, or others associated with the deceased, had enough concern for the latter’s posthumous wellbeing to later invest in a mud-plastered and painted carapace after the body had been disturbed and dismembered,” wrote Sowada and her colleagues.
    We don’t know this person’s name, but the remains offer silent proof of how seriously their family took their beliefs about the afterlife and duty to one’s ancestors. The postmortem misadventures of the remains also remind us that grave robbing was an ancient profession.
  To repair the damage done during the robbery, the embalmers re-wound the linen wrappings, and then they plastered a thin layer of mud over the cloth to create a protective shell around the damaged mummy. When it dried, the mud helped hold the broken limbs in place and preserve the shape of an intact body. Add a few more layers of linen over the mud shell, and hey, presto, the deceased is as good as new and ready to carry on with the afterlife.
 Archaeologists had never seen this technique before, which is what caught Sowada's attention when she first noticed the thin layer of mud during a CT scanning session back in 1999. Twenty years later, improvements in CT scanning technology allowed Sowada and her colleagues to get a more detailed look. The resolution of the recent scan allowed the archaeologists to distinguish between individual layers of linen, and between linen and the painted mud shell. The results confirmed that they were looking at a mummification trick no one had seen in thousands of years.
 The basic mechanics of mummification didn't change much over the millennia: remove the gooey parts, dry out the body, apply preservative chemicals, and wrap the body in linen to help preserve it. But like any technology and/or cultural practice, the details evolved over time, and they also varied depending on where you lived and how rich and important you were.
 Around the 21 st Dynasty (1294 to 945 BCE), some royal mummies included a layer of linen soaked in resin, which dried into a hard shell. Seti I and his son Rameses II both received this treatment. Sowada and her colleagues suggest that the painted layer of mud could have been an affluent-but-not-blue-blooded person's attempt at copying high society. But if that's the case, it's hard to see why embalmers would save it for the repair attempt. Perhaps they remembered the resin technique and thought, "Something that dries into a hard shell would be perfect for this postmortem restoration, but what's less astronomically expensive than resin?"
 It's also possible that encasing mummies in mud was standard practice during a window of time, at least for some social classes. Archaeologists don't have enough information to know yet, but Sowada and her colleagues suspect that NMR.27.3 wasn’t the only mummified person to receive this treatment, just the first one archaeologists noticed.
 “Understanding how common this practice had become in the late New Kingdom will require the radiological study and publication of further non-royal mummified individuals from this era,” wrote Sowada and her colleagues.
     One thing is certain, however: NMR.27.3  definitely wasn't a New Kingdom noblewoman named Meruah, who died around 1000 BCE. Her names and titles are painted on the lid of the wooden coffin, but the mummified body inside isn’t actually Meruah. Radiocarbon dating and the style of the scenes painted on the coffin suggest that she died somewhere between 945 and 1040 BCE. On the other hand, a radiocarbon date of the mummy’s linen wrappings suggests that the person  inside the coffin died sometime between 1207 and 1113 BCE. Meruah probably hadn’t even been born yet.
 Archaeologists will probably never find Meruah’s body, but they’re pretty sure how a stranger ended up in her coffin: colonialism.
 Sometime in the past, looters probably broke into Meruah’s tomb and stole her coffin. Later—much later—shady 19 th century antiquities dealers decided they could charge more for the fancy coffin if it had a mummy inside, so they found one. And apparently they were right, because a wealthy British tourist named Sir Charles Nicholson bought Meruah’s coffin and its unknown occupant on a trip to Egypt in 1856.
  The person now occupying Meruah’s coffin may or may not have been female. Several years ago, DNA sequenced from the remains suggested that the person was genetically male, but Sowada and her colleagues say that in their CT scans, the person’s pelvis, skull, and lower jaw look more like the bones of a woman.
 “We are assuming the differences in the results of the skeletal examination and the aDNA result stem from a problem with the aDNA study,” Sowada told Ars. “Without further investigation, we are assuming the issue relates to contamination somewhere in the process.”
 The painted mud itself only adds to the uncertainty. Sowada and her colleagues examined fragments of the mud shell with X-ray fluorescence and Raman spectroscopy—both techniques that measure the chemical composition of a sample based on how its molecules reflect light or X-rays. It turns out to be a fine-grained brown clay, coated with a white calcite pigment and then painted red.
 Although the mud covers the mummified person’s entire body, the samples Sowada and her colleagues examined came from near the head; a colleague of Sowada’s carefully removed three small fragments in 1999. Sowada and her colleagues don’t know yet whether the rest of the shell was also painted. “I am interested to know if the whole carapace was painted and even decorated; we will have to wait for further improvements in imaging technologies to answer this question,” Sowada told Ars.
 From the 19 th to the 21 st dynasty—around the time NMR.27.3 died—Egyptian artists tended to paint men with reddish faces and women with yellow ones. That’s just the way things were done, and Egyptian art tended to be very conventional and stylized. Death masks and coffins followed tradition, with men’s faces being painted in reddish pigments like the one on NMR.27.3’s shell.
 But in Thebes, near modern-day Luxor, archaeologists have found some examples of women depicted with darker reddish-brown skin. Since Meruah’s coffin is a Theban style, and Nicholson bought the coffin and the mummified person inside it near Luxor, the red pigment could also mark the deceased as a woman. After all, conventions change depending on when and where you are.
title: 古代的防油工使用泥土将受损的木乃伊抱在一起
title_en: Ancient embalmers used mud to hold a damaged mummy together

note:
信息周三披露，亚马逊司机将受到安装在亚马逊送货车辆上的摄像头的持续监控。亚马逊制作的信息视频详细介绍了由初创公司Netradyne设计的系统如何工作。
 驾驶员监控系统安装在挡风玻璃后面的车顶上，并具有四个摄像头。三个指向车辆外部，第四个指向驾驶员。借助计算机视觉软件，该系统将能够检测车辆内部和外部的潜在危险情况。
 例如，如果驾驶员运行停车牌，系统将对其进行检测，向驾驶员发出声音警告，并将素材上传到亚马逊的服务。如果驾驶员走得太快或跟着其他车辆太近，也会提醒他们（并会上传镜头）。该系统还可以检测驾驶员是否正在注视着他们的智能手机或正在方向盘上入睡。
 在其他情况下（包括急刹车，急转弯和掉头），系统将上传素材，而不会警告驾驶员。
    亚马逊在其视频中强调，该系统不会录制音频，也无法进行实时监控。亚马逊表示，在沿着路线行驶时，主管永远不会远程监视驾驶员。车辆停止时，驾驶员可以禁用面向驾驶员的摄像头。
 亚马逊认为，相机有时对驾驶员有帮助。例如，如果另一辆车撞上了亚马逊汽车，则摄像机拍摄的镜头可能证明亚马逊司机没有过错。
  亚马逊认为该系统将改善道路安全。错误的驾驶员将获得反馈（无论是直接从监视系统获得反馈，还是事后获得主管的反馈），并有望改善他们的驾驶行为。 
长期以来，安全倡导者一直呼吁在所有类型的车辆中使用更好的驾驶员监控系统，但由于消费者的强烈反对，减缓了私人车辆的普及。 一些汽车制造商（包括作为其“超级巡航”驾驶员辅助技术的一部分的凯迪拉克）已为其汽车增加了基本的驾驶员监视功能。  像亚马逊这样的雇主更有力地坚持认为，他们的司机采用这种技术可以提高安全性，并可能在此过程中减少亚马逊的法律费用。 Netradyne只是出售类似技术以用于商用车队的几家公司之一。  早在2017年，我就参观了销售类似系统的初创公司Nauto的总部。 去年，我们撰写了有关Eyesight Technologies（一家以色列公司）的信息，该公司开发了一种商用驾驶员监控系统，即使驾驶员戴上口罩也可以使用。
note_en:
Amazon drivers will be subject to constant monitoring by cameras installed onboard Amazon delivery vehicles, The Information  revealed on Wednesday. An  Amazon-made informational video details how the system, designed by startup Netradyne, will work.
 The driver-monitoring system is installed on the roof just behind the windshield, and it has four cameras. Three are pointed outside the vehicle, and the fourth is pointed at the driver. With the help of computer-vision software, the system will be able to detect potentially dangerous situations both inside and outside the vehicle.
 For example, if a driver runs a stop sign, the system will detect it, issue an audio warning to the driver, and upload footage to Amazon's services. Drivers will also be alerted (and footage will be uploaded) if they go too fast or follow other vehicles too closely. The system can also detect if drivers are looking at their smartphones or falling asleep at the wheel.
 In other cases—including hard braking, sharp turns, and U-turns—the system will upload footage without alerting the driver.
    In its video, Amazon emphasizes that the system doesn't record audio and doesn't have the capability for real-time monitoring. Amazon says that supervisors are never going to be remotely watching drivers as they travel along their routes. Drivers can disable the driver-facing camera when the vehicle is stopped.
 Amazon argues that the cameras can sometimes be helpful to drivers. For example, if another vehicle crashes into the Amazon vehicle, footage captured by the cameras could prove that the Amazon driver wasn't at fault.
  Amazon argues that the system will improve on-road safety. Bad drivers will get feedback—either directly from the monitoring system or from a supervisor after the fact—and will hopefully improve their driving behavior.
 Safety advocates have long called for better driver-monitoring systems in all types of vehicles, but worries of a consumer backlash have slowed adoption in personal vehicles. A few carmakers—including Cadillac as part of its Super Cruise driver assistance technology—have added basic driver-monitoring capabilities to their cars.
 Employers like Amazon are in a stronger position to insist that their drivers adopt technology like this that could improve safety and perhaps reduce Amazon's legal costs in the process. Netradyne is just one of several companies selling technology like this for use in commercial vehicle fleets.
 Back in 2017, I visited the headquarters of  Nauto, a startup that sells a similar system. Last year, we  wrote about Eyesight Technologies, an Israeli company that has developed a commercial driver-monitoring system that works even when drivers wear face masks.
tags: 人工智能,亚马逊,amazon,摄像头,cameras
title: 亚马逊将使用摄像头和人工智能来监控送货司机
title_en: Amazon will use cameras and AI to monitor delivery drivers

note:
据报道，Parler的联合创始人兼首席执行官John Matze通过2020年爆炸性增长和2021年甚至更具爆炸性的去平台化来控制公司发展。
 该公司董事会上周五罢免了前高管，《华尔街日报》是第一篇报道。玛兹（Matze）在一份声明中说，他“不断抵抗”。对他的＆＃34;产品愿景，他对言论自由的坚定信念，以及他对应该如何运行Parler的看法，并补充说，他主张提高产品稳定性，并且我认为这是一种更有效的内容审核方法。 Matze声称在＆＃34;天＆＃34;内被罢免时Parler重新上线的经历。
  Matze最初进行内容审核的方法（即没有任何内容）是上个月使Parler陷入热水之中，并最终将其从整个Internet上踢了下来。
 派勒（Parler）于2018年以＆＃34; （即基本上不受限制）替代Facebook和Twitter。到2019年和2020年，它吸引了一些政治保守派和右翼用户，这些用户大多（错误地）认为其他平台（尤其是Twitter）压制了保守派言论。
  在去年11月的美国总统大选期间及之后，极端分子蜂拥至Parler，其他平台（如Reddit，Twitter和Facebook）则禁止分享与QAnon相关的内容以及对选举结果的虚假说法。今年早些时候，在华盛顿特区，关于Parler的呼吁暴力和流血的帖子增多了，该平台的几位用户都呼吁并积极参加1月6日美国国会大厦的暴动，造成5人死亡，数百人受伤。
 国会骚乱发生两天后，谷歌就将Parler从Android应用商店中撤出，理由是该平台未能审核包含明显威胁或暴力呼吁的帖子。苹果第二天也效仿，出于类似原因，将Parler从iOS App Store中撤出。对Parler的最后一击是在同一周末，当时Amazon从AWS Web Hosting引导它，使其完全脱机。
    帕勒（Parler）在天黑时向亚马逊提起诉讼，以该科技巨头在停止向帕勒（Parler）提供服务时违反了反托拉斯法和合同法为由，请求法院恢复该程序。但是，此案随后的文件清楚表明，亚马逊已经就网站上的一些令人反感的内容向Parler发出了几个月的警告，而法官拒绝了Parler立即恢复原状的请求。 
他说，Matze希望Parler能够快速重新上线，并将其恢复到Google和Apple移动应用程序商店。为此，他提议在平台上扩大自动内容审核的范围，并全面禁止与指定的国内恐怖组织有联系的人员和团体，并补充说，有很多新纳粹团体属于该类别类别。＆＃34;
 保守的脱口秀节目和在线人物丹·邦吉诺（Dan Bongino）对Parler进行了投资，并担任董事会成员，驳回了Matze的要求。
 ＆＃34; John决定公开，而不是公开给我们，＆＃34; Bongino在他的在线脱口秀节目中说。他补充说，如果我们只屈膝的话，我们本可以在一个星期内起床。亚马逊，苹果和谷歌。但是，他和董事会其他成员显然对与技术平台见面没有兴趣。条款。 ＆＃34;公司的愿景是成为一个免费的演讲网站和一个稳定的产品，能够免疫并坚决消除文化，这是我们的。
 美国东部标准时间下午4:11更新：发布了这个故事后，Parler首席政策官艾米·佩科夫（Amy Peikoff）向Ars发了一条声明，描述了Matze将其解雇的特征描述为“不准确且具有误导性”。
 Parler领导层不懈地努力构建一个致力于表达自由，公民话语权和用户隐私的弹性，无党派的平台，＆＃34;佩科夫说。在今天争取言论自由的斗争中，她称Parler为灯塔。并表示该公司正在积极地进行重新启动，并希望很快会欢迎用户回来。＆＃34;
title: 帕勒（Parler）首席执行官表示，董事会因计划禁止“新纳粹”组织而解雇了他

note_en:
Today's Dealmaster includes a couple good deals on LG's acclaimed CX series of OLED TVs, with  the 55-inch model down to $1,350 on Amazon when you clip the on-page coupon. While this isn't the absolute lowest price we've seen for this variant—it dipped about $10 lower in a brief sale last December—it's not far off, and it's about $200 off the typical price we've seen online in recent months.
 That's still expensive, and  new models will arrive later this year. But those newer sets appear to be relatively modest upgrades, and  the current CX is  still highly regarded for offering tremendous contrast (which is a staple of all good OLED panels), excellent HDR performance, and  HDMI 2.1 support with gamer-friendly features like variable refresh rate. If you prefer a larger size, the  65-inch model is on sale for $1,950, which again isn't the lowest price we've ever seen but is lower than we've seen in recent months. The usual risks of OLED panels still apply—peak brightness isn't  the best for bright rooms, and static images can burn-in if left onscreen for severely extended periods—but either way this is a nice price for those in search of a premium TV for the near-term.
 Besides that, our deals roundup includes solid discounts on Amazon's Kindle Paperwhite, which we still consider the  best e-book reader for most people, Fitbit activity trackers, recommended noise-canceling headphones from Anker and Sony,   Ring Fit Adventure for the Nintendo Switch, and much more. 小型ISP的游说团体正在敦促联邦通信委员会调查SpaceX是否可以兑现其宽带承诺，并考虑阻止卫星提供商的农村宽带资金。同时，SpaceX表示，Starlink Beta现在正在为10,000个用户提供高速宽带服务。
 SpaceX是FCC农村数字机会基金（RDOF）的最大赢家之一，在10年中赢得了8.851亿美元，将Starlink宽带带到35个州的642,925个家庭和企业。总体而言，反向拍卖为全国180个实体提供了92亿美元（每年9.2亿美元）的资金，用于将网络扩展到520万户目前无法使用现代宽带速度的家庭和企业。
 但是获胜者仍必须提交＆＃34;大型应用程序＆＃34;请在1月29日之前提供有关他们打算用来履行义务的资格，资金和网络的其他信息。 FCC将审核这些申请，以确定是否应撤销任何资金。
 提供宽带的电力合作社对SpaceX的低地球轨道（LEO）卫星技术和固定无线服务都感到担忧，后者从地面塔楼向客户天线提供互联网访问。家园。全国农村电力合作社协会（NRECA）和全国农村电信合作社（NRTC）向FCC提交了一份白皮书，声称RDOF裁​​决使“美国农村地区”的宽带希望面临风险。
  正如彭博社今天的一篇文章所述，NRECA的首席执行官坚决反对SpaceX的资金：
 SpaceX的在轨宽带技术是一项完全未经验证的技术，全国农村电力合作社协会首席执行官吉姆·马西森（Jim Matheson）表示，该协会的成员争夺该资金。 ＆＃34;为什么要用这笔钱进行科学实验？＆＃34;
 根据NRECA的数据，提供宽带的电力合作社从FCC拍卖中获得了总计16亿美元的收入，可为31个州的90万个地点提供服务。其中包括180个合作社，这些合作社是五个财团的一部分，该财团的总收入约为15亿美元。和五个个人电动合作社[共]赢得了5940万美元。 
对FCC系统的搜索显示，所有这些电气合作社都在FCC的千兆位竞标。 NRECA表示＆＃34;很多＆＃34;的电力合作社正在使用光纤技术来提供这些速度，并在FCC拍卖中推动了更高的标准，以确保具有优质服务的合作社可以与服务速度较慢或参差不齐的其他类型的Internet提供商竞争在农村地区。＆＃34;
    Matheson在一份文件中告诉FCC，许多LEO卫星奖和固定无线奖都授予了在电子合作社服务范围内的人口普查区块。
  SpaceX承诺在FCC的“高于基准”范围内提供服务。层，需要100Mbps的下载速度，20Mbps的上传速度，以及每月至少2TB的数据上限。根据SpaceX Starlink Beta测试人员的早期报告，该服务可以提供比FCC的100ms标准更好的高速和延迟。 SpaceX仍在继续发射卫星，并已告诉Beta测试人员，期望在未来几个月内速度，延迟和正常运行时间会持续改善。在竞标这笔资金之前，SpaceX首先必须克服FCC的要求。严重的疑问关于它是否可以提供所需的延迟。
 Starlink的性能不是理论上的还是实验上的， SpaceX昨日在一份无关的FCC文件中表示。 ＆＃34;如今，在美国和国外有10,000多个用户正在使用该服务。作为其公开Beta版计划的一部分，其性能正在实时地迅速提高，但Starlink网络已经成功地证明了它可以超越委员会的“高于基准”标准；和“低延迟”性能等级。＆＃34;
 Starlink已经提供了100Mbps的下载速度和20Mbps的上传速度，并且在95％的网络往返延迟测量中提供了31ms或以下的延迟。该公司说。
  但是反对SpaceX的FCC资助的组织表示，该技术尚未得到证实，因为该技术尚未广泛使用。 ＆＃34;根据FCC规则，所有看起来不太可能向所有位置提供承诺速度的应用都应取消资格。 NRECA / NRTC白皮书说。
 卫星LEO卫星服务目前正在beta测试中，并且在极其有限的区域内只能在有限的基础上进行商业销售，问题仍然存在。目前，基于LEO的宽带缺乏以一定速度和延迟组合执行的演示功能。委员会合理地要求，＆＃34;小组写道。 ＆＃34;竞标未经过验证的LEO卫星服务是直接矛盾＆＃34;他们还说，有关FCC的要求。 （SpaceX是唯一一家获得资助的LEO卫星公司。） 
白皮书质疑LEO卫星是否可以在成千上万的订户签署服务时始终如一地提供高速度。他们暗示说，SpaceX甚至不需要资金，而卫星提供商最终计划将服务交付到各个地区，而不管他们是否获得补贴。
 我们今天与SpaceX联系了有关组的信息。提交，如果我们得到回应，将更新本文。
     至于固定无线，小组认为提供千兆速度是可能的，但是只有在适当的条件下才可以。 NRTC表示，其与农村公用事业部门合作的经验表明，由于多种原因，在美国农村地区，这种速度的条件基本上无法满足。
 固定无线的挑战包括在较低的频率上需要大量频谱带宽以进行传播，需要较高的垂直资产以获取较高的频谱，从发射机到天线的视线接近或绝对视线以及为以下目的大量部署光纤电缆回传目的，＆＃34;他们说。 ＆＃34;分配给固定无线竞标者以提供千兆位服务的支持的许多区域可能无法满足这些条件，或者实现起来的成本过高。
 FCC农村宽带资金由美国人通过对电话费收取的费用来支付。
  并非只有ISP游说团体对SpaceX资金感到担忧。消费者权益组织Free Press研究了拍卖结果，发现SpaceX在诸如“泽西城目标商店”（Jersey City Target store）等令人惊讶的地方赢得了融资。普查区与豪华酒店＆＃34;在芝加哥； ＆＃34;空的停车场，草地和高速公路中位数在华盛顿特区附近；一个位于迈阿密海滩市中心的停车场，距离海滩只有两个街区，四周都是多家提供千兆位服务的公司。和旧金山的一条街道，与金门公园的南边缘接壤。 SpaceX似乎已遵守规则。但是FCC的规则创建了一个损坏的系统，该小组说。
 目前尚不清楚，FCC是否可能撤回授予SpaceX或其他公司的任何或全部资金。但是FCC代理主席杰西卡·罗森沃尔（Jessica Rosenworcel）批评时任主席阿吉特·派（Ajit Pai）进行拍卖，而没有等待FCC收集更准确的宽带数据。 
＆＃34;在部署之前，我们需要先绘制地图，而在部署之前需要先绘制数据，＆＃34; Rosenworcel在2020年1月做出决定时说。根据今天的决定，我们将在未来十年内承诺提供160亿美元的绝大多数通用服务资金，而无需首先采取任何措施来改善地图，准确测量服务或解决我们遇到的数据灾难关于今天的服务状态。这意味着，如果今天的FCC地图将您的房屋标记为已服务，而现在则没有，那么在接下来的十年中，您将独自一人。 （FCC最终在该基金的第一阶段拨款了92亿美元，而不是最高160亿美元。在尚未计划的第二阶段中可能有112亿美元可用。）
 鉴于Rosenworcel的观点，RDOF的第一阶段是否进行了某些更改（无论是SpaceX的资金还是其他人的更改）也就不足为奇了。 FCC最近收到了参议员Shelley Moore Capito（R-WV）的来信，他反对授予Frontier Communications资金，因为该公司过去未能满足宽带部署要求。在Pai于1月卸任之前，由157个国会议员组成的两党联盟致信FCC，以确保每个资助ISP的技术，财务，管理，运营技能，能力和资源都得到保障。无论他们使用什么技术，都可以为他们计划服务的每个美国人提供他们承诺的服务。＆＃34;
